So you've got your meeting notes, but then those connect to your email, to Slack, and to all your other integrations so that you can run these really complex, you know, actions from within the platform or Slack or wherever you're looking to integrate with it.

So I would say the folks that are the most successful with read, I think, really value the accuracy and the robustness of the output that they get, whether it be from the meeting notes or from Search Copilot, I think we get a lot.
of really positive feedback about the accuracy, whether it be the transcription, the action items, if you're doing a more comprehensive search, I think that is a really key feature.

Secondarily, we've got really robust controls around sharing, joining, and where you can have your meetings, how we integrate with Google, how you can control sharing across your teams, how you can organize your notes.

So this becomes, for again, back to like our power users, if you think about how much information is in your meetings and then living in Notion and living in Slack, being able to connect all those dots and look for patterns or trends or summaries or what's the status of this or where are my benefits or, you know, things like onboarding new users, that's a really great use case for something like a search copilot.

I guess if we look at Glean outside of like the amount of professional services and costs that Glean takes to get up and running.
Yeah.
Is there any features or kind of competitive sets that Glean has that you guys are still working towards?
I think the thing that we would say when we compete with Glean is we're more of a bottoms up Glean.
So Glean, you have to implement from the top down with this huge cost and huge overwhelming support.
The way we implement...
Is you only have access to what your system access is.
So, for example, if I connect Google Drive to my search copilot, I only have access to the files on Google Drive that I have access to.
So just because our whole company has Google Drive doesn't mean I can now read, you know, David's email or David's documents on Google Drive.
still holds my permissions.
And so it's very easy to implement from the bottom up as a team than something like a glean that's got to be top down with permissioning.
So that's kind of how we're different and better than glean.
From a functionality perspective, I think we're pretty much the same.
There's not anything uniquely different that I've heard about glean.
Gotcha.
I've never used it, but some people that I've talked to have used it, so they like it.

A platform like Lean.

more.
